Front-End Developer & UI/UX Designer

ADITYA

I build interfaces that make ambitious products feel inevitable — engineered for performance, designed for clarity.

Available for projects0shipped case studiesNext.js / React / TypeScript / Three.js / Framer Motion

Studio

01 / Studio

I build interfaces the way engineers build systems — and refine them the way designers refine type. Every project starts from the same question: what does this interface need to do, and what is the fastest, clearest way to let it do that.

01

Engineering-first

Component architecture, state, and rendering strategy are decided before a single pixel is polished. Performance is a design constraint, not a post-launch fix.

02

Systemic design

Typography, spacing, and motion are treated as a system with rules — not one-off decisions per screen. Consistency compounds; it's built in from the first component.

03

Motion with intent

Animation is used to clarify hierarchy and state, never to decorate. If a transition doesn't help the user understand what changed, it doesn't ship.

Services

02 / What I Do
01

Front-End Engineering

Production-grade builds in React, Next.js and TypeScript — accessible, type-safe, and tuned for real-world performance budgets.

02

UI / UX Design

Interface and interaction design from wireframe to high-fidelity system — grounded in usability, not just visual polish.

03

Motion & Interaction

Physics-based micro-interactions and page choreography built with Framer Motion, GSAP, and WebGL — purposeful, never gratuitous.

04

Design Systems

Component libraries and documentation that let a team ship fast without drifting from the source of truth.

Capabilities

03 / Where the Depth Is

Front-End Engineering

Motion & Interaction (3D / WebGL)

Performance Engineering

UI / UX Design

Design Systems

A self-assessed view of where the depth is, not a scorecard — the case studies below are the actual evidence.

How I Work

04 / Process
01

Discovery

Understand the product, the users, and the constraints before any design or code — what does this interface actually need to do?

02

Planning

Map the component architecture, data flow, and rendering strategy up front, so performance and structure are decided by design, not discovered in review.

03

Design

Wireframe to high-fidelity system, grounded in the same typography, spacing, and motion rules that will ship — no disposable mockups.

04

Development

Type-safe, component-driven builds in React and Next.js, built against real data and real constraints from day one.

05

Testing

Cross-browser and cross-device checks against real-world conditions — not just the happy path on a fast connection.

06

Deployment

Staged rollouts with monitoring in place — shipping is a step in the process, not the end of it.

07

Support

Iteration doesn't stop at launch — fixes, refinements, and feature work as the product and its users evolve.
